chapter_title.en: The Reward of the Night Prayer

text: 1372 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى يُحِبُّ اَلْمُدَاعِبَ فِي اَلْجَمَاعَةِ بِلاَ رَفَثٍ اَلْمُتَوَحِّدَ بِالْفِكْرِ اَلْمُتَخَلِّيَ بِالْعِبَرِ اَلسَّاهِرَ بِالصَّلاَةِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1372 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, loves one who engages in light-heartedness in a gathering without indecency, one who reflects deeply while alone, one who contemplates lessons from life in solitude, and one who stays awake for prayer."
